headline: Johor's special economic zone gives state power to override 5 federal laws
context: The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone agreement grants Johor state authority to override five federal regulations on labour, customs, immigration, taxation, and environmental standards.

cards.big: Johor's SEZ allows state to override 5 federal laws — constitutional or convenient?
cards.sub: Special economic zone creates regulatory enclave within Malaysian territory

cards.big: SEZ Act 2025 grants exemptions from Employment Act, Customs Act, and 3 other statutes
cards.sub: Workers in SEZ have fewer legal protections than workers elsewhere in Malaysia

cards.big: SEZ aims to attract RM100 billion in investment over 10 years
cards.sub: Economic promise used to justify governance compromises and worker protection rollbacks

cards.big: State-federal coordination committee has only advisory capacity, no veto power
cards.sub: Federal government cannot override SEZ decisions without amending the enabling act

cards.big: Economic zones that override labour and environmental law aren't development — they're deregulation
Should special economic zones be allowed to weaken worker protections in exchange for investment?
